commit.message:
Address PR #1 review: topic keywords and flag thresholds (#10)

* address PR #1 review: expand topic keywords, calibrate flag thresholds from data

- Expand TOPIC_KEYWORDS with domain terms (medicaid, medicare, veterans,
  broadband, transit, etc.) to reduce coverage gaps (Mike's comment on PR #1)
- Replace hardcoded flag thresholds with percentile-based calibration
  computed from actual data distributions at fit time
- Restrict high_scrutiny flag to DOGE-flagged contracts only, since
  scrutiny score is only meaningful for items DOGE has acted on

* remove fallback thresholds, require calibration from real data

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Centralize shared constants and fix review findings

- Move QUERY_TO_TOPIC to config.py (was duplicated in preprocess.py
  and naive_baseline.py with divergent entries)
- Use config.py path constants (RAW_DATA_DIR, PROCESSED_DATA_DIR)
  instead of hardcoded relative paths in all scripts
- Clamp negative savings to 0 in scrutiny score calculation
- Fix lease normalization: stop mapping location to vendor_recipient
- Trim jargon list to actual buzzwords (removed "framework",
  "solution", "strategic", etc. that appear in real contracts)
- Fix mutable default argument in evaluate() (list → None)
- Replace 2.718281828 ** x with math.exp(x) in naive_baseline.py
- Add missing newline at EOF in four files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Michaelsaju1 <153621782+Michaelsaju1@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(011): wizard bug fixes — back-nav, resume, cursor, labels, summary, docs (#114)

* docs(clarify): update 011-wizard-bug-fixes spec with clarifications

Refs: #90, #91, #92, #93, #94, #66, #103
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(plan): add implementation plan for 011-wizard-bug-fixes

- Fill plan.md with full Phase 0 research and Phase 1 design
- Add research.md: 7 research findings (focus detection, cursor restore,
  resume exit guard, history pre-population, dimColor, summary gaps, docs)
- Add data-model.md: StepNavProps changes, extractStepValues mapping,
  ConfigSummary additions, cursor restore pattern, resume state guard
- Add quickstart.md: per-bug implementation checklist with commit order
- Add contracts/wizard-ux.md: back-nav, resume, cursor restore UX contracts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(tasks): generate task breakdown for 011-wizard-bug-fixes

27 tasks across 10 phases covering 7 user stories:
- US1 (P1): focus-aware back nav — StepNav + wizard + contexts + app-config (T003–T008)
- US2 (P1): resume exit guard + history pre-population (T009–T012)
- US3 (P1): cursor restore via process signal handlers (T013–T014)
- US4 (P2): remove dimColor from optional step (opt) label (T015)
- US5 (P2): add browser + aiTools to config summary (T016–T017)
- US6 (P2): contexts language/version multi-select + validation (T018–T021)
- US7 (P2): update getting-started.md + config-reference.md for spec 010 (T022–T023)

Refs: #90, #91, #92, #93, #94, #66, #103

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wizard): resolve back-nav, resume, cursor, label, summary, and version validation bugs

- StepNav: add isInputFocused + onAtFirstStep props; guard useInput when input focused
- wizard: add extractStepValues() for resume history pre-population; clamp resume step to LAST_STEP; show first-step b-key hint; remove dimColor from (opt) sidebar label
- config-summary: add Browser and AI Coding Tools conditional sections
- index: restore terminal cursor on exit, SIGINT (130), SIGTERM (143)
- contexts: versionError state + inline validation for lang-version-manual phase
- tests: +25 tests covering StepNav, extractStepValues, resume clamping, ConfigSummary, LanguageBindingSchema
- docs: update getting-started.md to 13-step STEP_REGISTRY; update config-reference.md with correct browser/aiTools/versionManagers/schemaVersion shapes

Closes #90, #91, #92, #93, #94

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(implement): mark 011-wizard-bug-fixes implemented

Closes: #90, #91, #92, #93, #94

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(analyze): add consistency report for 011-wizard-bug-fixes

- Mark T003–T026 as complete [X] in tasks.md
- Add deferral notes to T006, T007, T014
- Correct T017 test file reference (config-summary.test.ts)
- Note T018/T019 as pre-existing-no-change
- Fix plan.md R6 browser field shape to { selected: string[], default: string|null }
- Advance spec.md status from Implemented → Analyzed

Refs: #90, #91, #92, #93, #94

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(wizard): clarify extractStepValues configurations overlap comment

Steps 6 and 7 both carry 'configurations' in their history frames
because the Tools step and Editor Config step both write to that field.
Each frame must contain it so back-nav to either step restores the
correct partial state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(step-nav): remove orphaned isInputFocused and onAtFirstStep props

StepNav is not imported by any step component. Focus-safe back-nav for
text inputs is handled per-component via GateInput's !editing guard in
contexts.tsx. The first-step hint is wired at the wizard level via its
own useInput call — not through StepNav.

Remove the two unused props, simplify useInput back to always-active,
and document the per-component back-nav pattern in the file header.
Update wizard-navigation tests to drop the onAtFirstStep render test.

Closes #113

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(analyze): update tasks.md to reflect StepNav prop cleanup (T003b)

Mark T003/T004 as superseded by post-analysis refactor.
Add T003b tracking the isInputFocused/onAtFirstStep removal (closes #113).
Update T008 description to match actual test coverage after removal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wizard): resolve 4 manual-test bugs

- Fix blank OS in config summary by initializing wizard config state
  with detectOS() so config.os is always populated from step 0
- Clear checkpoint when wizard fully completes (advance past LAST_STEP)
  so re-running tilde after a completed session no longer shows the
  stale 'Resume from step 12' prompt
- Remove now-redundant clearCheckpoint() from step-11 onComplete since
  the advance() function handles cleanup at the final step boundary
- Auto-select pre-installed AI tools on first visit to AI Tools step
  (savedNames absent → selected defaults to entry.installed instead of false)

Closes #100

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wizard): cast detectOS() to 'macos' to satisfy TildeConfig type

TildeConfig.os is z.literal('macos') — OS type ('macos'|'linux'|'windows')
is wider. The cast is safe since assertMacOS() guards the wizard entry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wizard): resolve resume UX, Finish hang, and config discovery

- app.tsx: Wizard onComplete now calls setDone(true) and renders a
  completion screen; removes stale 'process.exit is handled by
  ConfigExportStep' comment that was wrong
- wizard.tsx: resume prompt shows 'Edit configuration' when lastCompletedStep
  >= LAST_STEP (fully completed); shows 'Resume from step N' otherwise
- wizard.tsx: fix OS spread order so checkpoint partialConfig.os=undefined
  can't clobber the detectOS() default (initialConfig spread first, os last)
- apply.tsx: call onComplete() after 1.5s when Apply succeeds so wizard
  completes cleanly instead of hanging on the done screen
- writer.ts: writeConfig always writes a canonical copy to
  ~/.tilde/tilde.config.json so discoverConfig() finds the config
  regardless of where the dotfiles repo lives; dotfilesRepo param is
  now optional (falls back to ~/.tilde/)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wizard): add Edit/Start Over options to config-first mode

After completing the wizard and re-running tilde, the config-first
mode now shows three choices instead of one:
  - Apply this configuration (existing)
  - Edit configuration  → launches ReconfigureMode with existing config
  - Start over (run wizard) → launches fresh Wizard

app.tsx gains a configEditMode state ('apply' | 'edit' | 'start-over')
to select between the three rendering branches when a config is found.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(config-summary): show config file location in summary

Added optional configPath prop to ConfigSummary component, rendered
as a dimmed 'Config: <path>' row at the bottom of the summary box.

config-first mode passes the loaded configPath so users can see exactly
which file was used.  apply.tsx omits it (path not yet known before
apply runs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(011): update constitution, spec, tasks, and site docs for post-PR fixes

Constitution (2.4.0 → 2.4.1 PATCH):
- Schema version in Config export step: 1.5 → 1.6
- Entry Modes table: Config-first now documents Apply/Edit/Start Over/Cancel
- Principle IV: config-first confirmation MUST offer all four options
- Sync impact report updated

specs/011-wizard-bug-fixes:
- spec.md: added US8 (config-first Edit/Start Over), US9 (OS/AI/apply
  regressions); added FR-012–FR-018 and SC-008–SC-010
- tasks.md: Phase 10 T027 marked done; added Phase 11 with T028–T035
  covering all post-PR manual test bug fixes

site/docs/getting-started.md:
- Fixed config detection search order (cwd → git-root → ~/.tilde/)
- Added 'Re-running tilde' section documenting Apply/Edit/Start Over/Cancel
- Updated 'Updating your config' to lead with Edit configuration as primary
  flow, with tilde update as the targeted alternative

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tests): guard canonical config write in contract tests (H1)

- afterEach in config-schema.test.ts now deletes ~/.tilde/tilde.config.json
  after each test so contract runs no longer silently overwrite the
  developer's real config (review finding H1)
- apply.tsx: store setTimeout handle in completeTimerRef and clear on
  unmount to prevent stale-closure call if component unmounts early (M1)
- wizard.tsx: remove redundant currentStep dep from skip useCallback —
  skip only delegates to advance which already captures currentStep (M2)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(apply): pass explicit undefined to useRef to satisfy TS strict types

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: exclude .obsidian/ from version control

Obsidian is a local doc viewer — workspace state, appearance, and plugin
files are personal and change on every open. Plugin setup is documented
in docs/guides/obsidian.md instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(types): make hole-reporting item match exhaustive (#977)

Keep the unresolved-inference post-check pass fail-closed by
replacing the catch-all item arm with explicit safe variants.
This forces future parser Item additions to opt into auditing
instead of silently skipping hole reporting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update laststanding: BearSSL submodule replaces amalgamation

BearSSL can't be amalgamated (static symbol collisions), so it's now
a git submodule compiled as a separate static library (libbearssl.a).

Changes in Oscan:
- Remove embedded bearssl_amalg.c (deleted upstream)
- Embed 13 BearSSL public headers instead (~526KB total)
- Write headers to bearssl/inc/ subdirectory in temp dir
- Link libbearssl.a on Unix freestanding if found
- Windows TLS (SChannel) completely unaffected

Linux TLS requires libbearssl.a built from the submodule.
CI uses recursive submodules which pulls bearssl automatically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: Major project cleanup - CI fix and professionalization

**DELETED** (removed debug/temp files):
- FINAL_CLEANUP.bat, cleanup_final.py, execute_tasks.py
- test_stdin_bridge.py, run_stdin_test.bat
- Redundant docs (MASTER_INSTRUCTIONS.md, PHASE_2_ROADMAP.md,
  PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md, PROJECT_ORGANIZATION.md)
- validation/outputs/ (temporary test results)

**MOVED** (fixed CI issue):
- requirements.txt: config/ → root
- requirements-dev.txt: config/ → root
- Reason: CI/CD systems expect these at project root

**UPDATED**:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml: Removed requirements-dev.txt install
  (keep CI simple; only production deps needed)

**ADDED** (quick reference for users):
- PROJECT_STATUS.txt: One-page guide (simple English, no jargon)
- CLEANUP_SUMMARY.txt: Record of all changes
- README_CLEANUP.txt: Human-friendly explanation

**RESULT**: Project is now:
✅ Clean (no debug files)
✅ Professional (organized, well-structured)
✅ CI-Ready (requirements in correct location)
✅ User-Friendly (clear quick reference guide)
✅ Production-ready (ready to share publicly)

commit.message:
Add handoff normalizer, pre-compute architect context, fix stateless first-turn bloat

- Add _normalize_handoff_from_text(): reconstructs ---HANDOFF--- block from
  loose fields in agent response, avoiding costly retry re-sends
- Add _build_state_summary(): enriches architect's state with task counts,
  ready queue, and planning sufficiency — saves frontier tokens on mechanical
  analysis
- Fix stateless mode first_turn: use per-agent first_turn_done set instead of
  always treating stateless as first_turn (prevents full prompt re-injection
  every turn)
- Add 4 tests for handoff normalizer (loose fields, missing fields, parseability)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

analyzer: add net/http ReadRequest sentinel mapping

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Cache resolved edits for replay: avoid re-materializing on every replay

ensureCachedApplied() builds and caches the resolved edit list from
a full materialization. resolveReplayEdit() now scans the cached
list instead of replaying the entire history. Cache is invalidated
alongside cachedOrder when events are added or compacted.

Reduces cost of N replays from O(N * M) materializations to
1 materialization + N * O(S) scans (S = structural edits).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: deduplicate participant counts by personId in hub and home cards

Congregation and supervision participant counts now use unique personId
sets instead of raw cell_members doc count. Fixes inflated numbers when
a person belongs to multiple cells in the same congregation/supervision.

Affected: home_tab (supervision card, congregation card, admin list),
supervision_hub_screen, congregation_hub_screen.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Redesign Phase 3 (Campaigns) tab with opportunity/issue analysis

- Campaign selector (left) + Analysis filter (right)
- Shows ALL segments for campaign in unified table
- Opportunities: CPA <= avg_cpa/1.5 with >=2 conversions (sorted by CPA ascending)
- Issues: CPA >= avg_cpa*1.5 (sorted by CPA descending)
- Added Segment Type column for clarity
- Matches Phase 2 unified view design

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Mastery Engine — God-Tier Bug Hunting Intelligence

New package: bughunt/mastery_engine/ (5 files, 0 existing files modified)

Layer 1 — Elite Hypothesis Engine (hypothesis_engine.py):
  - Semantic parameter classification (9 intent types)
  - Contextual payload generation (IDOR, SSRF, Logic, Authz, etc.)
  - Every payload includes reasoning and PoC validation steps

Layer 2 — Adaptive Attack Strategist (attack_strategist.py):
  - Real state machine: RECON → FOOTHOLD → ESCALATE → PIVOT → IMPACT
  - Remembers captured data (tokens, IDs, endpoints)
  - Auto-generates context-aware next actions per state

Layer 3 — Parser Desync Engine (parser_desync.py):
  - Orange Tsai style URL confusion (12 techniques)
  - Hop-by-hop header abuse for auth bypass
  - CRLF/header injection detection
  - HTTP request smuggling indicators (CL.TE, TE.CL, TE.TE)
  - Encoding differential testing

Orchestrator — God Mode (god_mode.py):
  - Runs all 3 layers + feeds into Mythos reasoning
  - Full report generation with chain narratives

Usage: python -m bughunt.mastery_engine.god_mode --target URL

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Create PHASE_2_ROADMAP.md outlining professional enhancement plan:

Current Phase 1: 7/10 grade (hardware-ready, functional, validated)
Target Phase 2: 10/10 grade (automotive-grade professional standard)

9 professional tasks over 40-50 hours:
- PEP 8 & type hints for code quality
- Extended Kalman Filter for firmware optimization
- Collision probability analytics
- PDF safety audit reporting
- Dashboard UI/UX enhancements
- 90%+ test coverage expansion
- CI/CD automation with quality gates
- ISO 15623 safety compliance documentation
- Protocol verification process

Includes: Priority breakdown, implementation timeline (3-4 weeks),
resource requirements, risk mitigation, success metrics, dependency
graph, decision framework.

Establishes roadmap for team to maintain 10/10 professional grade.

- Task 1: PEP 8 & type hints (pylint ≥9.5/10)

- Task 3: Collision probability calculation & analytics
- Task 4: Dashboard UI/UX enhancements (risk gauge, safety score)

commit.message:
docs: add ConvNEF pipeline to technical report

Add Section 3.11 describing the gradient-free convolutional pipeline
architecture: PCA-based filter learning, multi-scale parallel stages,
spatial pyramid pooling, feature standardization, and ensemble
training.  All components are fully gradient-free.

Add Section 5.10 with comprehensive CIFAR-10 results from 7 sweep
rounds (80+ configurations on a T4 GPU).  Key results:
- PCA conv features lift flat-pixel 50.1% to 63.2% (+13.1%)
- Feature standardization is the largest single lever (+4.2%)
- Multi-scale parallel stages add +2.5% over single-scale
- 10-member ensemble with 30k neurons reaches 78.25%
- K-means filters, hierarchical stages, cutout, rectangular
  patches, and GCN all identified as negative results

Update Abstract to mention the 78.25% ConvNEF result.
Update Discussion 6.1 with ConvNEF competitive positioning.
Update strategy table (6.2) with ConvNEF row.
Update CIFAR-10 limitation (6.3) to reflect 78.3% vs prior 58%.
Add finding 13 to Conclusions covering ConvNEF.
Add Coates et al. (2011) reference for k-means filter comparison.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
